enrichment.short_description: Meltano is an open-source ETL platform with 600+ connectors for building version-controlled data pipelines.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Meltano is an open-source ETL platform with 600+ connectors, dbt integration, and a pay-per-compute Meltano Cloud tier.

features.title: 600+ pre-built connectors
features.description: Extract and load data using a large library of pre-built connectors for sources and destinations.

features.title: Extract & Load replication
features.description: Supports full, incremental, and log-based replication strategies for moving data.

features.title: dbt & Elementary integration
features.description: Built-in support for dbt transformations and Elementary data validation.

features.title: Pipelines as code
features.description: Pipeline configuration is version-controlled, treating data infrastructure as code.

features.title: Custom connector SDK
features.description: The Meltano SDK lets teams build their own custom connectors when a pre-built one doesn't exist.

features.title: Orchestration options
features.description: Pipelines can be orchestrated via Meltano Cloud or integrated with Apache Airflow, Dagster, or Orchestra.

pros: Free and open source at its core
Large library of 600+ connectors
Native dbt integration for transformations
Version-controlled, infrastructure-as-code pipeline configuration
Used by companies including GitLab, Netlify, and HackerOne
cons: Meltano Cloud pricing is based on compute hours, not published as flat dollar amounts on the pricing page
Aimed at data engineers, so it lacks a simplified point-and-click interface for non-technical users
Requires orchestration tooling (Airflow, Dagster, Orchestra, or Meltano Cloud) for scheduling

faqs.question: Is Meltano free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Meltano is open source and free to self-host. A paid managed offering, Meltano Cloud, is also available.

faqs.question: How many connectors does Meltano support?
faqs.answer: Meltano provides access to more than 600 pre-built connectors for data sources and destinations.

faqs.question: Does Meltano support data transformations?
faqs.answer: Yes, Meltano integrates with dbt for transformation and Elementary for data validation.

faqs.question: Who maintains Meltano?
faqs.answer: Meltano is an open-source project maintained by Matatika.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted ELT pipelines
use_cases.description: Data engineers build and version-control extract-and-load pipelines using the open-source core.

use_cases.title: Managed data pipelines
use_cases.description: Teams that don't want to manage infrastructure run pipelines on Meltano Cloud, billed by compute hours.
use_cases.audience: Data teams without dedicated infrastructure ops

use_cases.title: Custom connector development
use_cases.description: Teams needing a connector outside the 600+ library build one with the Meltano SDK.
use_cases.audience: Engineering teams with unique data sources

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Meltano?
paragraphs: Meltano is an open-source ETL (extract, transform, load) platform that helps data teams build and manage data pipelines using more than 600 pre-built connectors.

It treats pipeline configuration as code, supports full, incremental, and log-based replication, and integrates with dbt for transformations. A paid Meltano Cloud tier offers managed infrastructure billed by compute hours instead of data volume.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Meltano is for
paragraphs: Meltano is built for data engineers who want programmatic, version-controlled control over ELT pipelines rather than a purely point-and-click interface, and who may pair it with Airflow, Dagster, or Meltano Cloud for orchestration.

enrichment.short_description: Singer is a free, open-source specification for building Taps and Targets that extract and load data between systems.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Singer is a free, open-source ETL spec using JSON-based Taps and Targets to move data between databases, APIs, and files.

features.title: Taps for extraction
features.description: Taps are scripts that extract data from a source such as a database, API, file, or queue.

features.title: Targets for loading
features.description: Targets are scripts that receive data from any tap and load it into a destination.

features.title: JSON message protocol
features.description: Taps and Targets communicate over JSON and JSON Schema, so any tap can pair with any target.

features.title: Incremental extraction
features.description: Supports maintaining state between runs so pipelines can extract data incrementally.

features.title: Unix-style composability
features.description: Uses simple pipes rather than daemons or custom plugin systems, following a Unix-inspired design.

pros: Completely free and open source
Simple, composable Unix-pipe architecture
Ecosystem of 100+ community-built taps and targets
Works across databases, APIs, files, and queues
cons: No official hosted/managed version from Singer itself; Stitch offers a commercial managed option built on the spec
No built-in scheduling or orchestration layer
Requires technical setup to write or run taps and targets

faqs.question: Is Singer free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Singer is a completely free, open-source specification and set of tools.

faqs.question: What are Taps and Targets?
faqs.answer: Taps are extraction scripts that pull data from a source, and Targets are loading scripts that receive data from any tap and load it into a destination.

faqs.question: Is there a hosted version of Singer?
faqs.answer: Singer itself is not a hosted service, but it is sponsored by Stitch, a fully managed data pipeline product that can schedule Singer taps.

faqs.question: How many taps and targets are available?
faqs.answer: The Singer ecosystem includes over 100 community-built taps and targets.

use_cases.title: Custom ETL scripting
use_cases.description: Engineers write or reuse Taps and Targets to move data between a specific source and destination.

use_cases.title: Data consolidation
use_cases.description: Organizations use Singer components to consolidate data from multiple tools into a single warehouse.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Singer?
paragraphs: Singer is an open-source specification for building Taps (extraction scripts) and Targets (loading scripts) that communicate over a shared JSON message format.
Because any tap can work with any target, teams can mix and match components from a community ecosystem of 100+ taps and targets to move data between databases, APIs, files, and queues.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Singer is for
paragraphs: Singer suits data engineers who want a free, composable, Unix-style approach to writing ETL scripts, rather than a fully managed pipeline product.

enrichment.short_description: Mage is a data and AI workflow platform with an open-source core, an AI Sidekick, and a usage-based managed cloud from $100/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Mage.ai builds data and AI workflows with an AI Sidekick and Autopilot monitoring, plus a managed cloud starting at $100/month.

features.title: AI Sidekick
features.description: Generates workflows from natural-language descriptions.

features.title: Autopilot
features.description: Automatically monitors, validates, recovers, and optimizes production workflows.

features.title: AI-ready data output
features.description: Converts workflow outputs into trusted context for analytics and AI agents.

features.title: Data unification
features.description: Integrates sources such as Stripe and Salesforce with destinations such as BigQuery.

features.title: Governance controls
features.description: Provides visibility, permissions, approvals, and auditability over workflows.

features.title: Flexible deployment
features.description: Supports fully managed cloud, hybrid cloud, private cloud, and on-premises deployment models.

pros: Open-source core available
AI-assisted workflow creation from natural language
Autopilot handles monitoring and recovery of production workflows automatically
Deployment flexibility across cloud, hybrid, private, and on-premises
Used by enterprise customers including Samsung, IBM, JPMorgan Chase, and Nokia
cons: Entry managed plan uses usage-based billing ($0.50 per CPU core/hour and $0.50 per 4GB RAM/hour) on top of the $100/month base, which can be hard to predict
Enterprise, hybrid, private, and on-premises deployments require contacting sales for custom pricing
Limited public pricing detail beyond the entry-level managed plan

faqs.question: Is Mage open source?
faqs.answer: Mage has an open-source core, alongside fully managed cloud and enterprise deployment options.

faqs.question: How much does Mage cost?
faqs.answer: The self-serve managed plan starts at $100/month plus usage-based infrastructure costs of $0.50 per CPU core/hour and $0.50 per 4GB RAM/hour. Enterprise and hybrid/private/on-premises deployments use custom pricing.

faqs.question: Does Mage use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, Mage includes an AI Sidekick that generates workflows from natural-language descriptions and prepares AI-ready data for analytics and AI agents.

faqs.question: What deployment options does Mage support?
faqs.answer: Mage supports fully managed cloud, hybrid cloud, private cloud, and on-premises deployment models.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted pipeline building
use_cases.description: Teams describe a workflow in natural language and let the AI Sidekick generate it.

use_cases.title: Autonomous workflow operations
use_cases.description: Autopilot monitors and recovers production data workflows without manual intervention.

use_cases.title: AI agent data preparation
use_cases.description: Organizations convert raw workflow output into trusted, reusable context for downstream AI agents.

features: Usage-based infrastructure ($0.50 per CPU core/hour, $0.50 per 4GB RAM/hour)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Mage?
paragraphs: Mage is a platform for building and running data and AI workflows, with an AI Sidekick that generates workflows from natural-language prompts and an Autopilot feature that monitors, validates, and recovers production workflows automatically.

It has an open-source core and a managed cloud offering that starts at $100/month plus usage-based compute costs, alongside hybrid, private, and on-premises deployment options for larger organizations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Mage is for
paragraphs: Mage is aimed at data teams, operations teams, and enterprises that need to automate data workflows and prepare AI-ready data, including large organizations such as Samsung, IBM, JPMorgan Chase, and Nokia.

enrichment.short_description: Node-RED is a free, open-source, browser-based visual tool for wiring together hardware, APIs, and online services.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Node-RED is a free, open-source flow-based programming tool for wiring devices, APIs, and services, governed by the OpenJS Foundation.

features.title: Visual flow editor
features.description: A browser-based drag-and-drop editor for wiring together flows that automate tasks.

features.title: 5,000+ community nodes
features.description: A large library of community-built nodes and flows extends what Node-RED can connect to.

features.title: Event-driven Node.js runtime
features.description: Built on Node.js with a non-blocking, event-driven architecture.

features.title: Edge and IoT deployment
features.description: Runs on low-cost hardware such as Raspberry Pi, BeagleBone Black, and Arduino, as well as Android.

features.title: Cloud deployment options
features.description: Can be deployed locally via Docker/Node.js, or run on FlowFuse, AWS, or Microsoft Azure.

pros: Completely free and open source
Large ecosystem of 5,000+ community nodes and flows
Runs on inexpensive hardware for home automation and IoT edge use
Governed by the OpenJS Foundation with active project leadership
Mature project in active development since 2013
cons: No official Node-RED-run cloud hosting; commercial hosting is provided by third-party sponsor FlowFuse
Visual flow-based programming can become harder to manage for very complex logic
Community nodes vary in quality and maintenance since they are contributed by third parties

faqs.question: Is Node-RED free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Node-RED is free and open source, distributed via npm.

faqs.question: Who created Node-RED?
faqs.answer: Node-RED was created in early 2013 by Nick O'Leary and Dave Conway-Jones of IBM's Emerging Technology Services group, and they continue leading the project today.

faqs.question: What organization governs Node-RED?
faqs.answer: Node-RED is governed by the OpenJS Foundation, with FlowFuse as its current active sponsor.

faqs.question: What hardware can run Node-RED?
faqs.answer: Node-RED runs on Docker and standard Node.js installations, as well as low-cost devices such as Raspberry Pi, BeagleBone Black, and Arduino, plus Android.

use_cases.title: Home automation
use_cases.description: Hobbyists wire together smart-home devices and services using the visual flow editor.

use_cases.title: Industrial and IoT control
use_cases.description: Engineers deploy Node-RED on edge hardware to automate industrial and IoT processes.

use_cases.title: API and service integration
use_cases.description: Developers wire APIs and online services together without building custom backend glue code.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Node-RED?
paragraphs: Node-RED is a free, open-source, low-code programming tool for event-driven applications, offering a browser-based visual editor for wiring together hardware devices, APIs, and online services.
Created in 2013 by Nick O'Leary and Dave Conway-Jones at IBM, it is now governed by the OpenJS Foundation and supported by a library of more than 5,000 community-built nodes.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Node-RED is for
paragraphs: Node-RED serves hobbyists building home automation projects as well as engineers deploying industrial control and IoT edge solutions on hardware like Raspberry Pi and Arduino.

enrichment.short_description: Latenode is a visual workflow automation platform combining 5,500+ app integrations, AI models, and custom JavaScript code.
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan available; paid usage from approximately $10.80/month
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Latenode combines a visual workflow builder with 5,500+ integrations and built-in AI models, with a free tier and usage-based CPU pricing.

features.title: Visual workflow builder
features.description: A drag-and-drop canvas for building automations from simple 3-node flows up to complex multi-agent systems.

features.title: 1,200+ AI models and apps
features.description: Access to AI models including GPT-4o, Claude, and Gemini without managing separate API keys.

features.title: 5,500+ integrations
features.description: Pre-built connectors across platforms such as Salesforce, HubSpot, Slack, Notion, and Shopify.

features.title: Custom JavaScript nodes
features.description: Full custom-code capability inside workflow nodes for complex logic.

features.title: AI agents
features.description: Supports multi-agent architectures and agentic orchestration for autonomous processes.

features.title: Enterprise security
features.description: SOC 2 Type II, GDPR compliance, SSO, and RBAC are included across all plans.

pros: Free plan with 10,000 CPU seconds and 5 active workflows
Unified pricing covers integrations and AI model usage without separate API keys
Supports custom JavaScript for complex automation logic
Enterprise security features (SOC 2, GDPR, SSO, RBAC) included on all plans, not gated to top tiers
Positioned as lower-cost than Zapier, Make, and n8n
cons: Usage-based CPU-second billing can make cost estimation less predictable than flat-rate plans
The Managed Service tier starts at $199/month for hands-on implementation support
Legally registered as Nocode LTD with limited public company background information

faqs.question: Is Latenode free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Latenode offers a free plan with 10,000 CPU seconds and 5 active workflows per month.

faqs.question: Do I need my own AI API keys?
faqs.answer: No, Latenode provides access to 1,200+ AI models and apps, including GPT-4o, Claude, and Gemini, without requiring separate API credentials.

faqs.question: How is Latenode priced beyond the free plan?
faqs.answer: Paid usage is billed by CPU seconds, for example approximately $10.80/month for 100,000 CPU seconds, plus a Managed Service tier starting at $199/month.

faqs.question: Can I write custom code in Latenode workflows?
faqs.answer: Yes, Latenode supports full custom JavaScript code inside workflow nodes.

use_cases.title: Lead capture and routing
use_cases.description: Marketing and sales teams automatically capture and route leads across systems.

use_cases.title: AI-generated content workflows
use_cases.description: Teams use built-in AI models to generate content as part of an automation.

use_cases.title: Invoice and document processing
use_cases.description: Finance teams automate invoice processing and data extraction.

use_cases.title: AI chatbots with memory
use_cases.description: Teams build chatbots with persistent memory using Latenode's AI agent capabilities.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Latenode?
paragraphs: Latenode is a business process automation platform that pairs a visual drag-and-drop workflow builder with 5,500+ pre-built integrations and 1,200+ AI models, without requiring users to manage separate API keys.

It offers a free plan (10,000 CPU seconds, 5 active workflows), usage-based pay-as-you-go pricing, a $199/month managed service tier, and an embedded iPaaS option for SaaS companies, with enterprise security (SOC 2, GDPR, SSO, RBAC) included on every plan.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Latenode is for
paragraphs: Latenode serves both technical teams who want custom JavaScript and full integration access, and non-technical business teams across marketing, sales, finance, and operations who use pre-built automation solutions.

enrichment.short_description: KonnectzIT is a no-code integration platform for connecting apps through drag-and-drop automations called Konnectz.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: KonnectzIT connects 400+ apps with no-code drag-and-drop automations, offering a free-forever plan and paid tiers starting at $15/month.

features.title: Visual drag-and-drop builder
features.description: Build multi-app automations, called Konnectz, using a visual workflow builder.

features.title: 400+ app library
features.description: Connects CRMs, email tools, communication apps, payment platforms, calendars, project management, scheduling, and storage services.

features.title: Unlimited workflows on all plans
features.description: Unlimited Konnectz are available even on the free plan.

features.title: API and webhook connections
features.description: Apps not in the built-in library can be connected using APIs and webhooks.

pros: Free-forever plan available
Unlimited automations (Konnectz) even on the free tier
No-code visual builder requires no technical setup
Affordable paid tiers starting at $15/month
cons: Smaller app library (400+) compared to larger automation platforms like Zapier
Positioned primarily for small businesses rather than complex enterprise integration needs
Limited independently documented information on company founding and background

faqs.question: Is KonnectzIT free?
faqs.answer: KonnectzIT offers a free-forever plan with unlimited Konnectz (workflows), alongside paid Pro, Team, and Agency plans.

faqs.question: How much does KonnectzIT cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans start at $15/month for Pro, $39/month for Team, and $59/month for Agency.

faqs.question: How many apps does KonnectzIT support?
faqs.answer: KonnectzIT's library includes 400+ apps spanning CRMs, email, communication, payments, calendars, project management, scheduling, and storage.

faqs.question: Who is KonnectzIT built for?
faqs.answer: KonnectzIT is built for small businesses automating repetitive processes, with notable usage among founders, CEOs, and directors in e-learning and marketing.

use_cases.title: Small business process automation
use_cases.description: Small business owners automate repetitive tasks across CRM, email, and payment tools.

use_cases.title: Marketing workflow automation
use_cases.description: Marketing and e-learning teams connect apps to automate lead and content workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is KonnectzIT?
paragraphs: KonnectzIT is a no-code app integration platform that lets users build automations, called Konnectz, using a visual drag-and-drop builder across a library of 400+ apps.
It offers a free-forever plan with unlimited workflows, plus paid Pro ($15/month), Team ($39/month), and Agency ($59/month) tiers for higher usage and team needs.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who KonnectzIT is for
paragraphs: KonnectzIT is aimed at small businesses, with notable usage among founders, CEOs, and directors in e-learning, marketing, and internet-based businesses looking to automate repetitive tasks without heavy technical overhead.

name: SureTriggers
skip_reason: The official website (suretriggers.com) 301-redirects to ottokit.com with the announcement 'SureTriggers is now OttoKit' — the product has been rebranded under a different name, so it no longer operates as 'SureTriggers'.

enrichment.short_description: Inngest is a durable execution platform for building reliable workflows, AI agents, and background jobs directly in application code.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Inngest is a durable execution platform for reliable AI agents and workflows, with a free Hobby plan and Pro starting at $99/month.

features.title: Durable execution
features.description: Steps retry independently from their failure point without restarting the entire run.

features.title: Flow control
features.description: Rate limiting, per-tenant concurrency limits, and throttling for workflow execution.

features.title: Agent observability
features.description: Step-level tracing, replay functionality, and evaluation metrics for AI agents and workflows.

features.title: SDK-based integration
features.description: Embeds into existing applications via an SDK supporting Next.js, Node.js, and Python.

features.title: Compliance
features.description: SOC 2 Type II compliant, with a HIPAA BAA available for regulated industries.

pros: Free Hobby plan with no credit card required
Embeds directly into existing application code without new infrastructure
Step-level tracing, replay, and evaluation for debugging workflows and AI agents
SOC 2 Type II compliant with HIPAA BAA available
Used by companies including Replit, ElevenLabs, Cohere, SoundCloud, and TripAdvisor
cons: Pro plan pricing scales with usage (executions, concurrency, data ingested), which can raise costs at higher scale
Enterprise-only features like SAML, RBAC, and 90-day trace retention require contacting sales
SDK-based approach means it's built for developers working in code rather than a visual/no-code interface

faqs.question: Is Inngest free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Inngest has a free Hobby plan with no credit card required, including 50k executions and 500k events ingested per month.

faqs.question: How much does Inngest's Pro plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Pro plan starts at $99/month and includes 1M+ executions and 100+ concurrent executions.

faqs.question: When was Inngest founded?
faqs.answer: Inngest released its durable execution platform in 2022.

faqs.question: Where is Inngest based?
faqs.answer: Inngest is headquartered in San Francisco, with a remote-first team.

use_cases.title: AI agent orchestration
use_cases.description: Teams building AI agents use Inngest for durable execution and step-level observability.

use_cases.title: Background job processing
use_cases.description: Developers run reliable background jobs that survive failures and deployments.

use_cases.title: Event-driven workflows
use_cases.description: Teams build event-driven systems and scheduled jobs without managing separate infrastructure.

features: Granular tracing, metrics, alerts + 7 day trace retention

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Inngest?
paragraphs: Inngest is a durable execution platform that manages reliable execution of workflows, AI agents, and background jobs, embedding durability and observability into application code rather than requiring separate infrastructure.
Founded in 2022 and headquartered in San Francisco, it offers a free Hobby plan, a $99/month Pro plan, and custom Enterprise pricing, and is SOC 2 Type II compliant with a HIPAA BAA available.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Inngest is for
paragraphs: Inngest is built for developers and teams building AI applications, complex workflows, and systems that need reliable execution at scale, including those in regulated industries requiring compliance.

enrichment.short_description: Webhook.site generates free, unique URLs and email addresses to instantly inspect, test, and automate incoming HTTP requests and emails.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Webhook.site gives developers free unique URLs to inspect incoming webhooks and emails, with paid plans from $7.50/month.

features.title: Webhook testing
features.description: Receive and inspect incoming HTTP requests in real time at a unique URL.

features.title: Email capture
features.description: Generates unique email addresses for testing email integrations.

features.title: DNS monitoring (DNSHook)
features.description: Captures and inspects DNS queries for testing.

features.title: Request replay
features.description: Re-executes previously captured requests.

features.title: Workflow automation
features.description: A drag-and-drop workflow builder with AI capabilities for automating actions on incoming requests.

features.title: Native integrations
features.description: Connects to Google Sheets, Slack, AWS S3, Dropbox, SFTP, databases, and JavaScript.

pros: Free tier requires no signup for quick webhook testing
Supports both webhook and email testing use cases
Includes a built-in workflow/automation builder
Native integrations with common tools like Slack, S3, and Google Sheets
Request replay simplifies debugging
cons: Free tier is limited to 100 requests per unique URL and URLs expire
Permanent addresses and account protection require a paid plan
Advanced features like SSO, custom domains, and white-labeling are reserved for paid tiers

faqs.question: Is Webhook.site free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Webhook.site offers a free tier limited to 100 requests per unique URL, with URLs that expire after a set period.

faqs.question: How much do paid plans cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans start from $7.50/month and include unlimited requests and emails, permanent protected addresses, and up to 100,000 request history.

faqs.question: Can I test email integrations?
faqs.answer: Yes, Webhook.site generates unique email addresses so you can capture and inspect test emails.

faqs.question: Does Webhook.site support automation?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes a drag-and-drop workflow builder with AI capabilities for automating actions on captured requests.

use_cases.title: Webhook debugging
use_cases.description: Developers inspect incoming webhook payloads in real time during integration development.

use_cases.title: Email integration testing
use_cases.description: Teams generate disposable email addresses to test outbound email flows.

use_cases.title: Request-triggered automation
use_cases.description: Teams use the workflow builder to automate actions when specific requests are received.

features: Up to 100 requests per unique URL
URLs expire after a set period

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Webhook.site?
paragraphs: Webhook.site generates free, unique URLs and email addresses and lets you see everything sent there instantly, making it easy to test, transform, and automate incoming web requests and emails without building custom infrastructure.

The free tier allows up to 100 requests per URL before expiry, while paid plans starting at $7.50/month add unlimited requests, permanent protected addresses, and advanced features like workflows, SSO, and custom domains.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Webhook.site is for
paragraphs: Webhook.site is used by software developers, automation engineers, and organizations building integrations that depend on webhooks or email communications.

enrichment.short_description: Beeceptor lets developers instantly create mock REST, GraphQL, SOAP, and gRPC API servers without writing backend code.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Beeceptor creates instant mock API servers from OpenAPI, GraphQL, or WSDL specs, with a free plan and paid tiers from $10/month.

features.title: AI-powered mock generation
features.description: Generates mock servers from API specs including OpenAPI, WSDL, GraphQL SDL, and proto files.

features.title: No-code rule configuration
features.description: Set matching rules and custom responses without writing code.

features.title: Real-time request inspection
features.description: Inspect incoming requests live for debugging webhooks and callbacks.

features.title: Dynamic responses
features.description: Generate responses dynamically using request data and headers.

features.title: Schema-less CRUD storage
features.description: Built-in mock database supporting CRUD operations without a predefined schema.

features.title: Proxy and record mode
features.description: Switch between live and mock modes, recording real responses for later replay.

features.title: Network condition simulation
features.description: Simulate latency, timeouts, and errors for failure testing.

features.title: Local tunnel
features.description: Routes payloads to a developer's local machine during testing.

pros: Free forever plan with no signup barrier
Supports multiple API protocols: REST, GraphQL, SOAP, and gRPC
AI-assisted mock creation directly from API specs
Processes over 10 million requests daily at 99.9% uptime
20% discount for annual billing on paid plans
cons: Free tier is limited to 50 requests/day and 3 mock rules
Higher-usage tiers add per-million-request overage fees (e.g. +$40 per extra 1M requests on Scale)
Enterprise features like SSO, audit logs, and on-premise deployment require higher-tier or custom plans

faqs.question: Is Beeceptor free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Beeceptor has a free forever plan limited to 50 requests/day and 3 mock rules, with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What API protocols does Beeceptor support?
faqs.answer: Beeceptor supports mock servers for REST, GraphQL, SOAP, and gRPC APIs.

faqs.question: How much do paid plans cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans start at $10/month for Individual, $25/month for Team, and $99/month for Scale, with a custom-priced Enterprise tier and a 20% discount for annual billing.

faqs.question: How reliable is Beeceptor?
faqs.answer: Beeceptor reports processing over 10 million requests daily across 1,000+ mock servers launched daily, with 99.9% uptime.

use_cases.title: API development without a backend
use_cases.description: Developers mock APIs from specs to build frontend or integration code before the real backend exists.

use_cases.title: QA test automation
use_cases.description: QA teams simulate API responses, latency, and errors to test failure handling.

use_cases.title: Product demos
use_cases.description: Teams use mock servers to run product demos without depending on live backend systems.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Beeceptor?
paragraphs: Beeceptor is a platform for creating mock API servers without backend infrastructure, generating REST, GraphQL, SOAP, and gRPC mock servers from uploaded API specs or no-code configuration.
It offers a free forever plan (50 requests/day, 3 mock rules) and paid tiers from $10/month up to a custom Enterprise plan, and reports processing over 10 million requests daily at 99.9% uptime.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Beeceptor is for
paragraphs: Beeceptor is used by development and QA teams, software architects, and API-based companies looking to accelerate integrations, testing, and demos without depending on live backend systems.
